specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > III. The Extracellular Milieu: ECM and PNNs (L3/L4)
source_formulae: P0R04781: The ECM (Hyaluronan, Proteoglycans) provides structural support (L3), guides development, and regulates the diffusion of ions and neurotransmitters (L2).

P0R04782: Tensegrity Extension (L1/L3): The ECM links to the cytoskeleton via Integrins, extending the cellular tensegrity network. This allows mechanical forces (phonons) to be transduced across the tissue, supporting L4 synchronisation.
test_protocols: preserve 1. Composition and Function: source-accounting boundary

specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > III. The Extracellular Milieu: ECM and PNNs (L3/L4)
source_formulae: P0R04783: 2. Perineuronal Nets (PNNs): The Guardians of Criticality
P0R04784: PNNs are specialised ECM structures that primarily ensheath inhibitory interneurons (e.g., PV cells).

P0R04785: Regulation of Plasticity (L3): PNNs stabilise existing connections and restrict plasticity, closing the "Critical Period." | Maintaining E/I Balance (L4): PNNs are crucial for maintaining the E/I balance and stabilising the network in the Quasicritical regime (sigma1).
